pattern: Counting integers up to a bound that share a digit-level property is a job for digit DP: recurse over digit positions carrying just enough state — the running even/odd digit balance and the remainder modulo k — and turn a range count into the difference of two prefix counts.
hints: A number can only have an equal count of even and odd digits when it has an even number of digits, so most candidates are ruled out before divisibility even matters.

Instead of scanning every integer in the range, count how many qualifying integers lie in [1, high] and how many lie in [1, low - 1], and subtract; build each count with a digit-by-digit recursion rather than a loop over values.

Track (digit position, even-count minus odd-count so far, remainder modulo k, whether the prefix is still capped by the bound's own digits, whether a nonzero digit has appeared yet) and memoize on that state — leading zeros must not count as digits, and the total work only scales with the number of digits, not with high minus low.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two qualifying two-digit multiples of 3
statement example, only 10 qualifies when every divisor is accepted
statement example, single-value range that does not qualify
entirely single-digit range, no digit count can ever balance
low and high both at the smallest allowed value, with the loosest divisor
a narrow window pinned at the maximum allowed value
every value has an odd number of digits, so nothing can ever balance regardless of divisor
every two-digit value is 'divisible' by the loosest divisor, isolating the digit-balance condition alone

stress.description: random modest-width windows scattered across the full value domain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    span = max(0, min(size, 200000))
    low = rng.randint(1, 1000000000 - span)
    high = low + rng.randint(0, span)
    k = rng.randint(1, 20)
    return [low, high, k]

stress.description: narrow windows pinned near the maximum boundary with a variety of divisors
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    span = max(0, min(size, 200000))
    high = 1000000000 - rng.randint(0, span)
    low = max(1, high - rng.randint(0, span))
    k = rng.randint(1, 20)
    return [low, high, k]
